Ayah

الٓر ۚ كِتَـٰبٌ أَنزَلْنَـٰهُ إِلَيْكَ لِتُخْرِجَ ٱلنَّاسَ مِنَ ٱلظُّلُمَـٰتِ إِلَى ٱلنُّورِ بِإِذْنِ رَبِّهِمْ إِلَىٰ صِرَٰطِ ٱلْعَزِيزِ ٱلْحَمِيدِ (۱)
Alif, Lam, Ra. [This is] a Book We have sent down to you that you may bring mankind out from darkness into light, by the command of their Lord, to the path of the All-mighty, the All-laudable (1)

Key Points

The act of bringing people out of darknesses into light has been repeated many times in the Quran. Sometimes it is attributed to Allah: «اللَّه ولى الّذین آمنوا یخرجهم من الظلمات الى النّور»(1). Sometimes it is attributed to the Prophets: «أن اخرج قومك من الظلمات الى النّور»(2). And sometimes it is attributed to the Book: «كتاب انزلناه الیك لتخرج النّاس من الظلمات الى النّور». The comparison of disbelief (kufr), division, ignorance (jahl), polytheism (shirk), and doubt to "darknesses" (ظلمات) is because in those states, man is bewildered, just like when he is in darkness. Light is the means of sight, awareness, movement, and growth, just as all these are present in the Divine Book and the path of Allah. «كتاب... الى النّور... الى صراط العزیز»

1) Al-Baqarah, 257 2) Ibrahim, 5.

Messages

1- The Quran alone is not sufficient; a Divine leader is also necessary for the guidance of the people. «كتاب انزلناه الیك لتخرج»

2- The philosophy behind the revelation of the Divine Books and the appointment of the Prophets is the salvation of humanity from darknesses. «لتخرج الناس ...» From the darkness of ignorance to the light of knowledge, from the darkness of disbelief to the light of faith, from the darkness of division to the light of unity, and from the darkness of sin to the light of piety (taqwa).

3- The source material for propagation and guidance must be the Quran. «كتاب... لتخرج»

4- Darknesses are many, but the Light is one. The Truth is one, but falsehoods are numerous. «الظلمات... النور» (The word Nūr [Light] is singular, while the word Ẓulumāt [Darknesses] is plural.)

5- Prophets and Divine Books are means of guidance; the essence of guidance is by the permission of Allah. «باذن ربّهم»

6- The path of Allah is the path of honor, glory, and perfection. «الى صراط العزیز الحمید»

Explanations

Portrait of Surah Ibrahim

This Surah has 52 verses and was revealed in Mecca, although verses 28 and 29 concern those killed at Badr and were revealed in Medina. This Surah discusses topics related to the mission of the Prophets, sermons, glad tidings, and the supplications (du'as) of Prophet Ibrahim عليه السلام. According to narrations, reciting this Surah brings blessings, including safety from calamity, poverty, and madness. This Surah is named "Ibrahim" because it discusses matters concerning Prophet Ibrahim عليه السلام, and like the preceding Surahs—Yunus, Hud, and Yusuf—it begins with «الر». The first verse of these Surahs concerns the descriptions of the Quran.

Ayah

ٱللَّهِ ٱلَّذِى لَهُۥ مَا فِى ٱلسَّمَـٰوَٰتِ وَمَا فِى ٱلْأَرْضِ ۗ وَوَيْلٌ لِّلْكَـٰفِرِينَ مِنْ عَذَابٍ شَدِيدٍ (۲)
—Allah, to whom belongs whatever is in the heavens and whatever is on the earth. Woe to the faithless for a severe punishment (2)

Messages

1- The law and school of thought worthy of being followed belongs to the One who created the universe and to Whom existence belongs. «كتاب انزلناه - له ما فى السموات»

2- Denying a God to Whom everything belongs does not harm Him; it is the cause of punishment for the disbelievers. «ویل للكافرین من عذاب»

Ayah

ٱلَّذِينَ يَسْتَحِبُّونَ ٱلْحَيَوٰةَ ٱلدُّنْيَا عَلَى ٱلْـَٔاخِرَةِ وَيَصُدُّونَ عَن سَبِيلِ ٱللَّهِ وَيَبْغُونَهَا عِوَجًا ۚ أُو۟لَـٰٓئِكَ فِى ضَلَـٰلٍۭ بَعِيدٍ (۳)
—those who prefer the life of this world to the Hereafter, and bar [others] from the way of Allah, and seek to make it crooked. They are in extreme error. (3)

Key Points

Messages

1- Worldliness is the basis for disbelief, opposition to religion, and deviation. «یستحبّون الحیوة الدنیا... و یصدّون... فى ضلال بعید»

2- The source of deviations is the performance (actions) of man himself. «یستحبّون، یصّدون، یبغون»

3- Man possesses free will and can prefer one path over another and choose it. «یستحبّون»

4- Disbelievers pass through three stages:

First, they themselves become deviated through worldliness. «یستحبّون»

Then, through their actions, they prevent others from the path. «یصدون»

Finally, with all their might, they seek to deviate the path of those seeking truth. «یبغونها»

5- The disposition of disbelief is the factor that drives the seeking of deviation. «ویل للكافرین... یبغونها»

6- Misguidance has degrees and stages. The deeper it becomes, the more difficult it is to return to the truth. «ضلال بعید»

Ayah

وَمَآ أَرْسَلْنَا مِن رَّسُولٍ إِلَّا بِلِسَانِ قَوْمِهِۦ لِيُبَيِّنَ لَهُمْ ۖ فَيُضِلُّ ٱللَّهُ مَن يَشَآءُ وَيَهْدِى مَن يَشَآءُ ۚ وَهُوَ ٱلْعَزِيزُ ٱلْحَكِيمُ (٤)
We did not send any apostle except with the language of his people, so that he might make [Our messages] clear to them. Then Allah leads astray whomever He wishes, and He guides whomsoever He wishes, and He is the All-mighty, the All-wise. (4)

Key Points

The meaning of «لسان قوم» (the language of his people) in this Ayah is not merely the dialect or language of the people, because sometimes a speaker might speak the people's language, yet they do not fully understand his words. Rather, the intent is to speak easily and comprehensibly, in a way that the people understand the Divine message, just as He says in other verses: «یسرناه بلسانك»(3) (We have made the Qur'an easy in your tongue). Prophet Moses (عليه السلام) also asked Allah to remove the impediment from his tongue so that people might understand his speech. «واحلل عقدة من لسانى یفقهوا قولى»(4)

The purpose of Allah's misguidance is the deprivation of His grace from obstinate individuals. Otherwise, if Allah intended to directly mislead someone, He would neither send a Book nor a Prophet. In other verses, we read that Allah misguides the wrongdoers (ẓālimūn), the defiantly disobedient (fāsiqūn), and the extravagant (musrifūn). This means that a person, through sin and wrongdoing, prepares the ground for being deprived of guidance.

Some commentators have said that the agent (fā'il) of «یشاء» (wills) is the people, not Allah. That is, Allah misguides the one who wills to be misguided and guides the one who wills to be guided. Similar to the Ayah: «كُلاً نُمّد هؤلاء وهؤلاء...»(5) (We aid both these and those...) We help a group in the path they choose to follow.

The Prophet's propagation, which was in the language of his people, does not contradict the universality of the religion. This is because the content of the message has no specific language, and based on the Qur'an's instruction, a group must learn the religious teachings through deep understanding (tafaqqah) and migration (hijrah) and then teach them to others.

3) Maryam, 97. 4) Taha, 27 - 28. 5) Al-Isra, 20.

Messages

1- The Prophets were familiar with the culture and language of the people of their time and propagated the message according to their understanding. «بلسان قومه»

2- Allah establishes the conclusive proof (itmām al-ḥujjah) against the people. «بلسان قومه لیبین لهم»

3- The relationship between religious leaders and the people is emotional, sincere, and direct. «بلسان قومه»

4- Propagation must be simple, explicit, clear, and understandable to the general public. «بلسان قومه»

5- Allah's guidance and misguidance are based on wisdom. «یضل... و یهدى... و هو العزیز الحكیم»

Ayah

وَلَقَدْ أَرْسَلْنَا مُوسَىٰ بِـَٔايَـٰتِنَآ أَنْ أَخْرِجْ قَوْمَكَ مِنَ ٱلظُّلُمَـٰتِ إِلَى ٱلنُّورِ وَذَكِّرْهُم بِأَيَّىٰمِ ٱللَّهِ ۚ إِنَّ فِى ذَٰلِكَ لَـَٔايَـٰتٍ لِّكُلِّ صَبَّارٍ شَكُورٍ (٥)
Certainly We sent Moses with Our signs: ‘Bring your people out from darkness into light and remind them of Allah’s [holy] days. There are indeed signs in that for every patient and grateful [servant].’ (5)

Key Points

In narrations, the day of the advent of Hadrat Mahdi عليه السلام, the Day of Return (Raj'ah), and the Day of Resurrection have been mentioned as instances of "Ayyām Allāh" (Days of Allah). (6)

6) Noor al-Thaqalayn.

Messages

1- Prophets must possess miracles. «ارسلنا... بِایاتنا»

2- The general goal of the Prophets is one. In the first Ayah of this Surah, Allah tells the Prophet of Islam, and in this Ayah, He tells Hadrat Moses: Lead the people out of the darknesses and guide them toward the light. «اخرج قومك»

3- The first sphere of the Prophets' mission is their own people. «قومك»

4- The way for people to be saved from darknesses is through remembering Allah's wrath and power against the oppressors, and paying attention to His favors toward those who came before. «ذكّرهم بایّام اللَّه»

5- Paying attention to history creates the foundation for patience (sabr) and gratitude (shukr) in man. Remembering past calamities and bitterness and their removal compels man to be grateful. And paying attention to the steadfastness and victory of past nations invites man to patience and resistance. «ان فى ذلك لایات لكل صبار شكور»

6- All days are the days of Allah, but the day of the manifestation of Allah's power has a different significance. (The manifestation of His power in wrath upon the disbelievers and the manifestation of His grace upon the believers.) «ایّام اللَّه»

7- Any celebration, mourning, or special ceremony that commemorates the Days of Allah (Ayyām Allāh) is permissible. «ذكّرهم بایّام اللَّه» (Contrary to the view of the deviated Wahhabi sect.)

8- The believer exercises patience during hardship and gratitude during prosperity. «صبّار شكور»

9- Patience is the foundation for realizing the truth. «لایات لكل صبار»

Ayah

وَإِذْ قَالَ مُوسَىٰ لِقَوْمِهِ ٱذْكُرُوا۟ نِعْمَةَ ٱللَّهِ عَلَيْكُمْ إِذْ أَنجَىٰكُم مِّنْ ءَالِ فِرْعَوْنَ يَسُومُونَكُمْ سُوٓءَ ٱلْعَذَابِ وَيُذَبِّحُونَ أَبْنَآءَكُمْ وَيَسْتَحْيُونَ نِسَآءَكُمْ ۚ وَفِى ذَٰلِكُم بَلَآءٌ مِّن رَّبِّكُمْ عَظِيمٌ (٦)
When Moses said to his people, ‘Remember Allah’s blessing upon you when He delivered you from Pharaoh’s clan who inflicted a terrible torment on you, and slaughtered your sons and spared your women, and in that there was a great test from your Lord. (6)

Messages

1- By comparing bitter and sweet events, and times of freedom and persecution, let us recognize the value of Allah's blessings. «اذكروا نعمة اللَّه»

2- We should attribute victories to Allah, not to ourselves. «نعمةاللَّه علیكم اذ انجاكم»

3- Freedom is among the most important divine blessings. «نعمةاللَّه...اذ انجاكم»

4- The most important task of the prophets is fighting against tyrants (Taghuts) and saving the deprived and the oppressed (Mustad'afin). «انجاكم»

5- Courtiers and entourages play an important role in preserving the tyrannical system. «ال فرعون»

6- Trial and affliction are for the purpose of human training and are among the aspects of divine Lordship (Rububiyyah). «بلاء من ربكم»

Ayah

وَإِذْ تَأَذَّنَ رَبُّكُمْ لَئِن شَكَرْتُمْ لَأَزِيدَنَّكُمْ ۖ وَلَئِن كَفَرْتُمْ إِنَّ عَذَابِى لَشَدِيدٌ (۷)
And when your Lord proclaimed, “If you are grateful, I will surely enhance you [in blessing], but if you are ungrateful, My punishment is indeed severe.” ’ (7)

Key Points

This Ayah is the most important and explicit verse in the Quran regarding gratitude for blessings (shukr) or ingratitude (kufrān) for them. It is presented immediately after the Ayah concerning the blessing of freedom and the establishment of a divine government under the leadership of Prophet Moses (عليه السلام). This signifies that divine governance and a heavenly leader are the most important blessings, and if gratitude is not shown, Allah will afflict the ungrateful people with a severe punishment.

Gratitude for blessings has stages: A: Gratitude of the heart, which is recognizing that all blessings come from Allah. B: Gratitude of the tongue, such as saying "الحمدللَّه" (All praise belongs to Allah). C: Practical gratitude, which is achieved through performing acts of worship and spending one's life and wealth in the path of Allah's pleasure and serving the people.

Imam Sadiq (عليه السلام) said: Gratitude for a blessing is avoiding sin. He also said: Gratitude is recognizing that the blessing comes from Allah (not from one's own cleverness, knowledge, intellect, or the efforts of others), being content with what Allah has given him, and not using divine blessings as a means for sin. True gratitude is placing Allah's blessing in the path of Allah. (7)

Gratitude in the face of divine blessings is very insignificant and negligible. As Sa'di says: It is better for the servant to bring An apology for his own shortcomings to Allah's court, Otherwise, no one can fulfill What is worthy of His Divinity.

We read in a Hadith that Allah revealed to Moses (عليه السلام): Fulfill the right of My gratitude. Moses said: This is impossible, because every word of gratitude requires another act of gratitude. The revelation came: This very acknowledgment of yours, and your knowledge that everything is from Me, is the best form of gratitude to Me. (8)

According to narrations, whoever does not thank people has not thanked Allah either. «من لم یشكر المنعم من المخلوقین لم یشكر اللَّه». (9)

If we use Allah's blessing in an unlawful path, it is ingratitude (kufrān al-ni'mah) and a prelude to disbelief (kufr). «لئن كفرتم» (If you are ungrateful), «بدّلوا نعمة اللَّه كفراً» (They exchanged the blessing of Allah for disbelief). (10)

7) Tafsir Namūneh. 8) Tafsir Namūneh. 9) Bihār, Vol. 71, p. 44. 10) Ibrāhīm, 28.

Messages

1- It is Allah's tradition (Sunnah) to make gratitude a means for increasing blessings, and He has decisively declared this tradition. «تأذن ربّكم» (Your Lord announced).

Gratitude for a blessing increases your blessing, Ingratitude for a blessing takes it out of your hand.

2- Allah made gratitude necessary for our training, not because He Himself is in need of gratitude. «ربّكم» (Your Lord).

3- Through gratitude, not only do Allah's blessings upon us increase, but we ourselves also grow, multiply, and ascend. «لازیدنّكم» (I will surely increase you).

4- The punishment for ingratitude is not merely the removal of the blessing; rather, sometimes the blessing is not withdrawn but turns into a calamity (niqmah) and a gradual enticement (istidrāj) until the person slowly falls. «لان كفرتم انّ عذابى لشدید» (If you are ungrateful, My punishment is indeed severe).

Ayah

وَقَالَ مُوسَىٰٓ إِن تَكْفُرُوٓا۟ أَنتُمْ وَمَن فِى ٱلْأَرْضِ جَمِيعًا فَإِنَّ ٱللَّهَ لَغَنِىٌّ حَمِيدٌ (۸)
And Moses said, ‘Should you be faithless,—you and everyone on the earth, all together—indeed Allah is all-sufficient, all-laudable.’ (8)

Messages

1- In the method of invitation (Da'wah) and propagation, the matter should not reach the point of begging people (to accept). «ان تكفروا... فان اللَّه الغنى»

2- Our faith and disbelief, or our gratitude and ingratitude, have no effect on His essence. «لغنّى حمید»

If the entirety of creation becomes disbelievers, no dust will settle upon the skirt of His Majesty.

3- We should not place any obligation (or favor) upon Allah through our faith and deeds, for He has no need for our actions. «لغنّى حمید»

Ayah

أَلَمْ يَأْتِكُمْ نَبَؤُا۟ ٱلَّذِينَ مِن قَبْلِكُمْ قَوْمِ نُوحٍ وَعَادٍ وَثَمُودَ ۛ وَٱلَّذِينَ مِنۢ بَعْدِهِمْ ۛ لَا يَعْلَمُهُمْ إِلَّا ٱللَّهُ ۚ جَآءَتْهُمْ رُسُلُهُم بِٱلْبَيِّنَـٰتِ فَرَدُّوٓا۟ أَيْدِيَهُمْ فِىٓ أَفْوَٰهِهِمْ وَقَالُوٓا۟ إِنَّا كَفَرْنَا بِمَآ أُرْسِلْتُم بِهِۦ وَإِنَّا لَفِى شَكٍّ مِّمَّا تَدْعُونَنَآ إِلَيْهِ مُرِيبٍ (۹)
Has there not come to you the account of those who were before you—the people of Noah, ‘Ad and Thamud, and those who were after them, whom no one knows [well] except Allah? Their apostles brought them manifest proofs, but they did not respond to them, and said, ‘We disbelieve in what you have been sent with. Indeed we have grave doubts concerning that to which you invite us.’ (9)

Key Points

This Ayah is one of the instances of remembrance and attention to the "Days of Allah" (Ayyām Allāh), which was discussed in the fifth Ayah of this Surah. «و ذكرهم بایام اللَّه»

According to Shaykh Tusi in Tafsir al-Tibyan, shakk (doubt) differs from rayb (suspicion/misgiving). Shakk is natural hesitation, but rayb is a doubt accompanied by ill-will and accusation. A doubt that leads to investigation and acceptance of the truth is constructive and beneficial doubt, but the doubt raised in the face of clear proofs (bayyināt) and miracles is merely obstinacy and destructive doubt that prevents man from accepting the truth. «لفى شكّ ... الیه مُریب»

The phrase «فردّوا ایدیهم فى افواههم» (and they put their hands over their mouths) can be interpreted in several ways: A: The opponents silenced the truth-tellers by placing their hands over their mouths, choking their voices. B: The miracles and proofs of the prophets were so strong that it was as if they took the people's hands and placed them over their mouths, meaning that people's tongues were tied before the truth and they had nothing to say. C: The opponents of the prophets bit their fingers out of intense anger. D: The opponents of the prophets placed their hands over their mouths in the presence of the Prophet, meaning that they should be silent and not preach.

Messages

1- Fixed traditions and laws govern history and society by Allah, such that familiarity with one part of history can be a lesson for another part. «الم یأتكم..»

2- Parts of human history are unknown and unrecognized. «لایعلمهم الااللَّه»

3- All prophets possessed miracles. «جائتهم رسلهم بالبینات»

4- The disbelievers were not only opposed to the person of the prophets, but they were opposed to their school of thought (their message). «كفرنا بما اُرسلتم به»

5- The doubt of the disbelievers regarding the truthfulness of the religion does not stem from a position of investigation, but its source is pessimism and obstinacy. «شك... مریب»

Ayah

۞ قَالَتْ رُسُلُهُمْ أَفِى ٱللَّهِ شَكٌّ فَاطِرِ ٱلسَّمَـٰوَٰتِ وَٱلْأَرْضِ ۖ يَدْعُوكُمْ لِيَغْفِرَ لَكُم مِّن ذُنُوبِكُمْ وَيُؤَخِّرَكُمْ إِلَىٰٓ أَجَلٍ مُّسَمًّى ۚ قَالُوٓا۟ إِنْ أَنتُمْ إِلَّا بَشَرٌ مِّثْلُنَا تُرِيدُونَ أَن تَصُدُّونَا عَمَّا كَانَ يَعْبُدُ ءَابَآؤُنَا فَأْتُونَا بِسُلْطَـٰنٍ مُّبِينٍ (۱٠)
Their apostles said, ‘Is there any doubt about Allah, the originator of the heavens and the earth?! He calls you to forgive you a part of your sins, and grants you respite until a specified time.’ They said, ‘You are nothing but humans like us who desire to bar us from what our fathers used to worship. So bring us a manifest authority.’ (10)

Key Points

In the previous Ayah, the disbelievers said to the prophets: "تدعوننا" (You call us) – you call us to the way of God. But in this Ayah, the prophets said to them: "یدعوكم" (He calls you) – God calls you. This means our invitation is not from ourselves or towards ourselves, but rather it is from God and towards God. According to Zamakhshari and Maraghi; the promises of the Quran regarding the believers is the forgiveness of all sins: "یغفر لكم ذنوبكم" (He will forgive you your sins). But regarding others (non-believers being invited), it is the forgiveness of some sins: "یغفر لكم من ذنوبكم" (He will forgive you some of your sins).

Messages

1- Belief in Allah is innate (fitrī) and self-evident, and it is not worthy of doubt. "أفى اللَّه شكّ"

2- Alongside intellect and innate nature, love and invitation are necessary. "أفى اللَّه شكّ... یدعوكم لیغفر لكم"

3- Religion invites humanity to purity from contaminations. "یدعوكم لیغفر... ذنوبكم"

4- Allah desires the forgiveness (ghufrān) and pardon of human sins. "یدعوكم لیغفر لكم"

5- Repentance (tawbah) and obedience to Allah cause the delay of a person's appointed term (ajal). "یدعوكم... یوخركم الى اجل مسمّى"

6- Out of compassion, Allah invites to guidance, and out of grace, He grants a respite for choice until the end of life. "یدعوكم - یؤخركم الى اجل مسمّىً"

7- The lifespan of every human being is measured and determined beforehand. "الى اجل مسمّىً"

8- Ethnic and racial prejudices prevent the acceptance of the truth. "یعبد آباؤنا"

9- Family plays an important role in a person's beliefs and convictions. "یعبد آباؤنا"

10- Obstinacy and arrogance against the truth are an incurable ailment. All the prophets approached people with miracles, but the stubbornness of some people caused them to constantly demand a new miracle from the prophets that suited their whims. "فأتونا بسلطان مبین"

Ayah

قَالَتْ لَهُمْ رُسُلُهُمْ إِن نَّحْنُ إِلَّا بَشَرٌ مِّثْلُكُمْ وَلَـٰكِنَّ ٱللَّهَ يَمُنُّ عَلَىٰ مَن يَشَآءُ مِنْ عِبَادِهِۦ ۖ وَمَا كَانَ لَنَآ أَن نَّأْتِيَكُم بِسُلْطَـٰنٍ إِلَّا بِإِذْنِ ٱللَّهِ ۚ وَعَلَى ٱللَّهِ فَلْيَتَوَكَّلِ ٱلْمُؤْمِنُونَ (۱۱)
Their apostles said to them, ‘Indeed we are just human beings like yourselves; but Allah favours whomever of His servants that He wishes. We may not bring you an authority except by Allah’s leave, and in Allah let all the faithful put their trust. (11)

Key Points

Although this Ayah states: Allah bestows His favor upon whomever He wills and appoints him as a Prophet, the Wise God knows who possesses the capacity and worthiness for this great responsibility. «اللَّه اعلم حیث یجعل رسالته»(11) Allah knows best where to place His message (His prophethood). The disbelievers (Kuffar) and polytheists (Mushrikeen) had two arguments against the Prophets: 1. You are human beings just like us. 2. Bring us the miracle that we propose. The answer to these two arguments is provided in this Ayah: As for the fact that we are human beings like you, we accept that, but Allah has bestowed His favor upon us and revealed (Wahy) to us. However, as for our miracle being according to your desire, that cannot be, because we do not have the right to bring a miracle without the permission of Allah.

(11) Al-An'am, 124.

Messages

1- Although all human beings are alike in form and appearance, this does not mean that they are also alike in inner reality and spirituality. «ان نحن الاّ بشر مثلكم ...»

2- Role models and educators must share similar conditions with those they educate. (What is a point of strength for the Prophets was considered a point of weakness by some. If the Prophets were not from among the people themselves, possessing human needs, instincts, and problems, they could not truly be educators and role models for humanity.) «بشر مثلكم»

3- The Prophets did not exaggerate concerning themselves, saying: We are human beings just like you. «ان نحن الاّ بشر مثلكم»

4- The station of Prophethood is a Divine gift and favor. «یمُنّ»

5- Miracles occur by the command of Allah, not by the desire of the people. (Even if people do not expect a miracle, the Prophets bring it.) «الاّ باذن اللَّه»

6- Reliance (Tawakkul) is a prerequisite of faith. «فلیتوكّل المؤمنون»

7- The believer does not tire from the obstinacy and denial of opponents, but continues his path by relying upon Allah. «وعلى اللَّه فلیتوكّل المؤمنون»

Ayah

وَمَا لَنَآ أَلَّا نَتَوَكَّلَ عَلَى ٱللَّهِ وَقَدْ هَدَىٰنَا سُبُلَنَا ۚ وَلَنَصْبِرَنَّ عَلَىٰ مَآ ءَاذَيْتُمُونَا ۚ وَعَلَى ٱللَّهِ فَلْيَتَوَكَّلِ ٱلْمُتَوَكِّلُونَ (۱۲)
And why should we not put our trust in Allah, seeing that He has guided us in our ways? Surely, we will put up patiently with whatever torment you may inflict upon us, and in Allah let all the trusting put their trust.’ (12)

Key Points

Tawakkul (reliance) means taking an agent (or representative). An agent must possess four conditions: wisdom/competence, trustworthiness, power, and love. And no one but Allah possesses these conditions perfectly, so we should rely upon Him. Imam Riḍā عليه السلام said: The boundary of tawakkul is that, with faith in Allah, you fear no one. (12)

12) Tafsir Nur al-Thaqalayn.

Messages

1- The God Who guided will also protect. Therefore, we should rely only upon Him. «نتوكل على اللَّه و قد هدینا»

2- The path of the prophets is the path of Allah. (Instead of saying "His path" (subulahu), He said:) «سُبلنا» (Our paths), just as He says elsewhere: «قل هذه سبیلى ادعوا الى اللَّه على بصیرة» (Say: This is my way; I invite to Allah upon clear evidence.) (13)

3- The prerequisite for traversing the path of Allah is enduring hardships. «سبلنا - لنصبرنّ»

4- One must both rely upon Allah and show patience and resistance against opponents. «نتوكل على اللَّه... ولنصبرنّ»

5- A believer does not abandon his belief or action due to harassment and torture. «لنصبرنّ على ما آذیتمونا»

6- Only one who has a support can endure. «لنصبرنّ... على اللَّه نتوكل» 13) Yusuf, 108.

Ayah

وَقَالَ ٱلَّذِينَ كَفَرُوا۟ لِرُسُلِهِمْ لَنُخْرِجَنَّكُم مِّنْ أَرْضِنَآ أَوْ لَتَعُودُنَّ فِى مِلَّتِنَا ۖ فَأَوْحَىٰٓ إِلَيْهِمْ رَبُّهُمْ لَنُهْلِكَنَّ ٱلظَّـٰلِمِينَ (۱۳)
But the faithless said to their apostles, ‘Surely we will expel you from our land, or you shall revert to our creed.’ Thereat their Lord revealed to them: ‘We will surely destroy the wrongdoers, (13)

Key Points

| Question: Were the Prophets polytheists (mushrik) before their mission (bi'that), such that the disbelievers requested them to return to their religion? «لتعودنّ فى ملّتنا» (You must certainly return to our religion). Answer: Firstly, it is possible that the disbelievers meant the Prophets' return to their (the disbelievers') authority. Meaning, they were saying: even if you have faith, you must not rise up against us, and you and your followers must not deviate from our path. Secondly, if the word "عود" ('awd, return) were accompanied by the particle "إلى" (ila), it would mean returning to the original state. But since it is accompanied by the word "فى" (fi, in), what is meant is change and transformation, not a return to the original state; meaning, change your belief and accept our religion. Thirdly, perhaps the intention behind returning to polytheism (shirk) was the return of the Prophets' followers, not the Prophets themselves.

Messages

1- All the Prophets faced opposition. «قال الذین كفروا لرسلهم» 2- Threat and expulsion are the policies of illogical tyrants. «لنخرجنكم ...» 3- The disbelievers do not want the believers to have an independent identity. «لتعودنّ فى ملّتنا» 4- Threats must be answered with threats. «لنخرجنكم - لنهلكنّ» 5- The reason for destruction is oppression (zulm), not disbelief (kufr). «كفروا... لنهلكنّ الظالمین» 6- Oppression does not last, and the oppressor is ultimately destroyed. «لنهلكنّ الظالمین»

Ayah

وَلَنُسْكِنَنَّكُمُ ٱلْأَرْضَ مِنۢ بَعْدِهِمْ ۚ ذَٰلِكَ لِمَنْ خَافَ مَقَامِى وَخَافَ وَعِيدِ (۱٤)
and surely We will settle you in the land after them. This [promise] is for someone who is awed to stand before Me and fears My threat.’ (14)

Key Points

Allah the Almighty has promised that the oppressors will be destroyed and His chosen servants (Awliya') will rule. Since this promise has not yet been widely fulfilled, it will be realized during the reappearance (Zuhur) of Imam Mahdi عليه السلام. The Holy Quran has repeatedly promised that "the chosen servants of Allah will rule the earth and their enemies will be destroyed." Here we mention three instances: A: «انّ جُندَنا لهم الغالبون»(14) (Indeed, Our host, they are the victors.) B: «و لقد سبقت كلمتنا لعبادنا المرسلین انّهم لهم المنصورون»(15) (Our word has certainly gone forth for Our servants, the messengers, that they are the ones who will be granted victory.) C: «اَنّ الارض یرثُها عِبادى الصّالحون »(16) (The earth will be inherited by My righteous servants.) In any case, the victory of truth over falsehood and the establishment of the divine government by the men of God are among the definite and repeated promises of the Quran.

The Divine Essence is the source of all goodness and virtues, and is worthy of love. «و الّذین آمنوا اشدُّ حُبّاً للَّه»(17) (And those who believe are stronger in love for Allah.) What causes piety (Taqwa) is the station (Maqam) of Allah. Yes! If your friend is a judge and your case goes to court, and accountability is involved, you fear his court and his station of justice, even though he is your friend.

14) Saffat, 173. 15) Saffat, 171, 172. 16) Anbiya, 105. 17) Baqarah, 165.

Messages

1- The disbelievers consider the earth to be their zone of power display, unaware that the governance of the earth will fall into the hands of the prophets and the believers. In contrast to the threat of the disbelievers: «لنخرجنكم من ارضنا» (We will surely expel you from our land), is the promise of Allah: «لنسكننّكم الارض» (We will surely settle you in the land).

2- Allah has promised victory to His chosen servants. «لَنُسكننّكم»

3- The prophets did not come merely to state rulings and give admonition; they also came for governance. «لنسكننّكم»

4- The condition for victory over the oppressors is possessing piety (Taqwa). «خاف مقامى و خاف وعید»

5- Do not fear the threat of the disbelievers when they say: «لنخرجنكم» (We will surely expel you); fear the threat of Allah when He says: «خاف وعید» (Fear My threat).

Ayahs

وَٱسْتَفْتَحُوا۟ وَخَابَ كُلُّ جَبَّارٍ عَنِيدٍ (۱٥)
They prayed for victory [against the infidels], and every obstinate tyrant was defeated, (15)
مِّن وَرَآئِهِۦ جَهَنَّمُ وَيُسْقَىٰ مِن مَّآءٍ صَدِيدٍ (۱٦)
with hell lying ahead of him, [where] he shall be given to drink of a purulent fluid, (16)

Key Points

The word warā’ (وراء) means consequence and end, just as we say in Persian: "Behind this food is sickness." (18)

All the Prophets were awaiting victory. Noah says: «اَنّى مغلوب فانتصر» (19) "O God! I am overcome, so grant help." Other Prophets also sought conquest and victory from Allah: «ربّنا افتح بیننا و بین قومنا بالحقّ و انت خیر الفاتحین» (20) "Our Lord! Judge between us and our people in truth, for You are the best of those who judge." The disbelievers also used to ask the Muslims: "You say that we will be victorious one day, so when will that day arrive?" «مَتى هذا الفتح» (21) It is here that the Noble Qur’an speaks in many verses about the victory of the Prophets, the destruction of the disbelievers, and their punishment, decisively announcing to the disbelievers that that day will soon arrive.

The drinks of the inhabitants of Hell on the Day of Judgment are scorching, not thirst-quenching:

A: «سُقُوا ماءً حمیماً فقطّع امعاءهم» (22) They will be given to drink boiling water that cuts their intestines to pieces.

B: «یغاثوا بماءٍ كالمُهل یشوى الوجوه» (23) They will be served water like molten brass, whose heat will roast their faces.

But the inhabitants of Paradise live beside rivers of pleasant water and partake of purified milk and honey prepared for them by Allah, benefiting from all divine blessings. «و سقاهم ربّهم شراباً طهوراً» (24)

It is recorded in history: One day, one of the oppressors of Banu Umayyah named Walīd ibn Yazīd ibn ‘Abd al-Malik opened the Qur’an to learn about his future, and his eyes fell upon the verse «واستفتحوا...» He became angry, tore up the Qur’an, and composed a poem to this effect: O Qur’an! Do you consider me an obstinate tyrant and threaten me? Yes, I am that obstinate tyrant. Today I tear you up, so that on the Day of Judgment you may testify that Walīd tore me to pieces. (25)

18) Tafsir Namūneh. 19) Al-Qamar, 10. 20) Al-A‘rāf, 89. 21) As-Sajdah, 28. 22) Muhammad, 15. 23) Al-Kahf, 29. 24) Al-Insān, 21. 25) Tafsir Namūneh.

Ayah

يَتَجَرَّعُهُۥ وَلَا يَكَادُ يُسِيغُهُۥ وَيَأْتِيهِ ٱلْمَوْتُ مِن كُلِّ مَكَانٍ وَمَا هُوَ بِمَيِّتٍ ۖ وَمِن وَرَآئِهِۦ عَذَابٌ غَلِيظٌ (۱۷)
gulping it down, but hardly swallowing it: death will assail him from every side, but he will not die, and there is [yet] a harsh punishment ahead of him. (17)

Key Points

In this Ayah, a portrait of the punishment of the inhabitants of Hell is presented: A: The punishment of the inhabitants of Hell is bit by bit (gradual) so that their torment may be greater. B: Various punishments bring the inhabitants of Hell to the brink of death, but they do not die. C: The punishments have tougher stages and are increasing. The Quran uses several expressions to describe the punishment of the Day of Judgment: "Alīm" (painful), "Shadīd" (severe), "Ghalīẓ" (harsh/gross), "Aẓīm" (great), all of which point to the severity and greatness of the punishment. "Isāghah" means drinking water with desire and willingness. The phrase "لایكاد یسیغه" (lā yakādu yasīghuhu) means he never drinks it willingly. (26) The Noble Messenger صلى الله علیه وآله said: Whoever drinks wine, his prayer will not be accepted for forty days, and it is fitting that Allah should make him drink from the foul-smelling, putrid water of Hell. (27)

26) Tafsir Tibyān. 27) Tafsir Majma‘ al-Bayān.

Messages

1- The punishment of Hell is gradual and continuous. "یتجرّعه" 2- The inhabitants of Hell do not die, and the punishment of Hell does not cause death. "ماهوبمیت" 3- The punishment of Hell is increasing. "ومن ورائه عذاب غلیظ"

Ayah

مَّثَلُ ٱلَّذِينَ كَفَرُوا۟ بِرَبِّهِمْ ۖ أَعْمَـٰلُهُمْ كَرَمَادٍ ٱشْتَدَّتْ بِهِ ٱلرِّيحُ فِى يَوْمٍ عَاصِفٍ ۖ لَّا يَقْدِرُونَ مِمَّا كَسَبُوا۟ عَلَىٰ شَىْءٍ ۚ ذَٰلِكَ هُوَ ٱلضَّلَـٰلُ ٱلْبَعِيدُ (۱۸)
A parable of those who defy their Lord: their deeds are like ashes over which the wind blows hard on a tempestuous day: they have no power over anything they have earned. That is extreme error. (18)

Key Points

In the previous verses, the discussion was about the loss and deprivation of stubborn oppressors, «و خاب كلّ جبّار عنید» (and every obstinate tyrant failed). This verse explains that very loss. Allah converts the bad deeds of the believers into good deeds and merits under certain conditions, «یبدل اللَّه سیئاتهم حسنات» (28). However, the bad deeds of the disbelievers destroy their good deeds, «كرماد اشتدّت به الرّیح» (like ashes which the wind blows hard upon). And in other verses, it is stated: «حبطت اعمالهم» (29), meaning their deeds are wiped out. The Quran has raised the issue of the nullification and futility of the disbelievers' deeds in many verses. For example, He says: «و قَدمنا الى ما عملوا من عمل فجعلناه هباء منثورا» (30). We bring forth their deeds and render them futile. And if we read concerning the disbelievers: «فلانقیم لهم یوم القیامة وزنا» (31) (We will not establish any weight for them on the Day of Resurrection), this might be a reference to the same point.

28) الفرقان، 70. 29) الأعراف، 147. 30) الفرقان، 23. 31) الكهف، 105.

Messages

1- The deeds of the disbelievers are fire that leaves nothing behind but ashes. «كرماد»

2- Do not be dazzled or captivated by the deeds of the disbelievers, for they are futile and fruitless. «كرماد»

3- The arrogant disbeliever is far from repentance and salvation. «بعید»

4- Disbelief nullifies even good deeds. «كفروا... اعمالهم كرماد»

Ayahs

أَلَمْ تَرَ أَنَّ ٱللَّهَ خَلَقَ ٱلسَّمَـٰوَٰتِ وَٱلْأَرْضَ بِٱلْحَقِّ ۚ إِن يَشَأْ يُذْهِبْكُمْ وَيَأْتِ بِخَلْقٍ جَدِيدٍ (۱۹)
Have you not regarded that Allah created the heavens and the earth with justice? If He wishes, He will take you away, and bring about a new creation, (19)
وَمَا ذَٰلِكَ عَلَى ٱللَّهِ بِعَزِيزٍ (۲٠)
and that is not a formidable thing for Allah. (20)

Key Points

The Quran has stated several times: We did not create the heavens and the earth in vain or as a plaything. «ما خلقنا السموات والارض و ما بینهما لاعبین»(32). In another place, He says: «ما خلقنا السّماء والارض و ما بینهما باطلا ذلك ظنّ الّذین كفروا»(33). Yes, it is the disbelievers who imagine the creation of existence to be aimless and futile.

32) Ad-Dukhan, 38. 33) Sad, 27.

Messages

1- The creation of the heavens and the earth has been accomplished based on wisdom, expediency, and a worthy goal. «بالحقّ»

2- Allah has no need for our existence or non-existence; let us not become arrogant. «ان یشأ یذهبكم»

3- Existence and its continuation depend on the Divine Will and Decree. «ان یشأ»

4- The world without humanity is futile; even if We take you away, We will bring other humans so that the creation of the heavens and the earth is not in vain.(34) «یأت بخلق جدید»

34) Tafsir al-Mizan.

Ayah

وَبَرَزُوا۟ لِلَّهِ جَمِيعًا فَقَالَ ٱلضُّعَفَـٰٓؤُا۟ لِلَّذِينَ ٱسْتَكْبَرُوٓا۟ إِنَّا كُنَّا لَكُمْ تَبَعًا فَهَلْ أَنتُم مُّغْنُونَ عَنَّا مِنْ عَذَابِ ٱللَّهِ مِن شَىْءٍ ۚ قَالُوا۟ لَوْ هَدَىٰنَا ٱللَّهُ لَهَدَيْنَـٰكُمْ ۖ سَوَآءٌ عَلَيْنَآ أَجَزِعْنَآ أَمْ صَبَرْنَا مَا لَنَا مِن مَّحِيصٍ (۲۱)
Together they will be presented before Allah. Then those who were weak will say to the arrogant [leaders], ‘Indeed we were your followers. So will you avail us against Allah’s punishment in any wise?’ They will say, ‘Had Allah guided us, surely we would have guided you. It is the same to us whether we are restless or patient: there is no escape for us.’ (21)

Key Points

Burūz (بروز) means emergence or exit, and mubāriz (مبارز) refers to someone who exits the ranks of the army and declares his readiness to fight the enemy. Although people are usually upon the religion and method of their rulers, "النّاس على دین ملوكهم" (People follow the religion of their kings), in the Islamic management system, we do not have absolute obedience to the manager. "لاطاعة لمخلوق فى معصیة الخالق" (There is no obedience to a creature in disobedience to the Creator)(35). Therefore, the slogan "المأمور معذور" (The commanded is excused) is not correct.

35) Bihar, Vol. 10, p. 227.

Messages

1- On the Day of Judgment, all people will be present before Allah in one place. "جمیعاً"

2- The result of blind imitation and following is regret. "كنا لكم تبعاً فهل انتم مغنون"

3- The inhabitants of Hell only expect a reduction in a part of the torment, not the entirety of it. "من شى"

4- The guidance or misguidance of community leaders plays an important role in the happiness or wretchedness of the people. "لو هدانا اللَّه لهدیناكم"

5- One of the spiritual and psychological torments for corrupt leaders on the Day of Judgment is their admission of their own helplessness and wretchedness before their followers. "لو هدانا اللَّه لهدیناكم"

6- Those who said to the prophets in this world: It makes no difference to us whether you advise us or not. "سواء علینا اَوَعظت ام لم تكن من الواعظین"(36) will also say on the Day of Judgment: It makes no difference whether we scream or remain calm; in any case, we are afflicted by the torment. "سواء علینا أجزعنا ام صبرنا"

7- Escape from the Divine court and punishment is impossible. "ما لنا من محیص"

8- On the Day of Judgment, there will be much clamor, argument, and conversation among the disbelievers. "قال ....قالوا"(37)

9- On the Day of Judgment, no one can prevent the Divine torment. "فهل أنتم مغنون"

36) Ash-Shu'arā, 136. 37) The verses related to the conversation between the oppressed and the arrogant disbelievers on the Day of Judgment are also mentioned in Sūrat Saba' Ayah 31 and Sūrat Ghāfir Ayah 47.

Ayah

وَقَالَ ٱلشَّيْطَـٰنُ لَمَّا قُضِىَ ٱلْأَمْرُ إِنَّ ٱللَّهَ وَعَدَكُمْ وَعْدَ ٱلْحَقِّ وَوَعَدتُّكُمْ فَأَخْلَفْتُكُمْ ۖ وَمَا كَانَ لِىَ عَلَيْكُم مِّن سُلْطَـٰنٍ إِلَّآ أَن دَعَوْتُكُمْ فَٱسْتَجَبْتُمْ لِى ۖ فَلَا تَلُومُونِى وَلُومُوٓا۟ أَنفُسَكُم ۖ مَّآ أَنَا۠ بِمُصْرِخِكُمْ وَمَآ أَنتُم بِمُصْرِخِىَّ ۖ إِنِّى كَفَرْتُ بِمَآ أَشْرَكْتُمُونِ مِن قَبْلُ ۗ إِنَّ ٱلظَّـٰلِمِينَ لَهُمْ عَذَابٌ أَلِيمٌ (۲۲)
When the matter is all over, Satan will say, ‘Indeed Allah made you a promise that was true and I [too] made you a promise, but I failed you. I had no authority over you, except that I called you and you responded to me. So do not blame me, but blame yourselves. I cannot respond to your distress calls, neither can you respond to my distress calls. Indeed I disavow your taking me for [Allah’s] partner aforetime. There is indeed a painful punishment for the wrongdoers.’ (22)

Key Points

On the Day of Judgment, the sinner struggles to find an accomplice in crime and shift the blame for his deviation onto others. Sometimes he says: A bad friend led me astray. «لقد اضلّنى عن الذّكر»(38) Sometimes he says: Corrupt leaders corrupted me. «لولا انتم لكنّا مؤمنین»(39) Sometimes he blames Satan and considers him the agent of his misguidance, but Satan says: Do not blame me, «لا تلومونى...» I did nothing but whisper and invite. The deviation originated from yourselves. Satan's lack of authority over man is both confirmed by Allah and admitted by Satan himself. «ان عبادى لیس لك علیهم سلطان»(40) You have no authority over My sincere servants. In this Ayah, too, He says: « ما كان لى علیكم من سلطان»

(38) Al-Furqan, 29. (39) Saba, 31. (40) Al-Hijr, 42.

Messages

1- Divine promises are true and correspond to reality. «وعد الحقّ»

2- Satan deceives man with his promises. «و وعدتكم فاخلفتكم» As stated elsewhere: «یعدهم و یُمنیّهم و ما یعدهم الشّیطان الاّ غرورا»(41)

3- Satan does not compel man; rather, he whispers and invites. «دعوتكم»

4- Even Satan reproaches the corrupt person. «لوموا انفسكم»

5- Reproach is one of the proofs of human freedom and free will, «لاتلومونى ولوموا انفسكم» because a compelled person is not subject to blame.

6- We should not shift the blame for our sins onto Satan. «فلاتلومونى»

7- On the Day of Judgment, relationships are severed. «ما انا بمُصرِخكم» (Leaders of falsehood and their followers disavow one another. Satan says to his followers: «انى كفرت بما اشركتمون» and the misguided followers also say to their leaders: If we were given another chance and returned to the world, we too would disavow you, just as you disavowed us. «لو انّ لنا كرّةً فنتبرّأ منهم كما تبرّءُوا منّا»(42))

8- Obedience to other than Allah is polytheism (Shirk). «اشركتمون»

9- Responding to Satanic invitations is oppression against oneself and the faith (Maktab). «انّ الظالمین لهم» (41) An-Nisa, 120. (42) Al-Baqarah, 167.

Ayah

وَأُدْخِلَ ٱلَّذِينَ ءَامَنُوا۟ وَعَمِلُوا۟ ٱلصَّـٰلِحَـٰتِ جَنَّـٰتٍ تَجْرِى مِن تَحْتِهَا ٱلْأَنْهَـٰرُ خَـٰلِدِينَ فِيهَا بِإِذْنِ رَبِّهِمْ ۖ تَحِيَّتُهُمْ فِيهَا سَلَـٰمٌ (۲۳)
Those who have faith and do righteous deeds will be admitted into gardens with streams running in them, to remain in them [forever], by the leave of their Lord. Their greeting therein will be ‘Peace!’ (23)

Key Points

Wherever you look in Paradise, there is peace (salām): Peace from Allah to the inhabitants of Paradise: «سلام قولا من ربّ رحیم»(43) Peace from the angels to the believers: «سلام علیكم طبتم فادخلوها»(44) Peace from the inhabitants of Paradise to one another: «تحیّتهم فیها سلام» Sometimes a person goes somewhere himself, and sometimes others come and take him there with special ceremonies. Allah says in this Ayah: «و اُدخِلَ» (and they will be admitted), meaning the inhabitants of Paradise do not enter simply and ordinarily; rather, they are taken to Paradise with respect and ceremony. In Ayah 73 of Surah Az-Zumar, He also says: «وسیق الّذین اتقوا ربّهم الى الجنّة زُمرا». But regarding the inhabitants of Hellfire, we read that they are driven towards the punishment with torment: «خذوه فغلّوه ثمّ الجحیم صلّوه»(45) (Seize him and shackle him, then cast him into the Hellfire). The inhabitants of Hellfire despise and detest one another and curse each other, but the inhabitants of Paradise exchange greetings (salām). In Paradise, there are various rivers: A: Rivers of water: «انهار من ماء» B: Rivers of milk: «انهار من لبن لم یتغیّر طعمه» C: Rivers of heavenly wine: «انهار من خمر لذة للشّاربین» D: Rivers of honey: «و انهار من عسل مصفّىً»(46)

43) Yā Sīn, 58. 44) Az-Zumar, 73. 45) Al-Ḥāqqah, 30-31. 46) Muḥammad, 15.

Messages

1- Believers who perform good deeds will remain in Paradise forever. «ادخل الذین امنوا... خالدین»

2- The inhabitants of Paradise are people of purity, sincerity, and peace (salām), not conflict or estrangement. «تحیتهم فیها سلام»

Ayah

أَلَمْ تَرَ كَيْفَ ضَرَبَ ٱللَّهُ مَثَلًا كَلِمَةً طَيِّبَةً كَشَجَرَةٍ طَيِّبَةٍ أَصْلُهَا ثَابِتٌ وَفَرْعُهَا فِى ٱلسَّمَآءِ (۲٤)
Have you not regarded how Allah has drawn a parable? A good word is like a good tree: its roots are steady and its branches are in the sky. (24)

Key Points

The pure tree (Shajarah Tayyibah) and the good tree have characteristics, such as: having growth, being fruitful, providing shade and being stable, and bearing fruit at all times. The monotheistic human is also never stagnant, and the effects of his faith are constantly manifest in his speech and actions. His belief is permanent, not seasonal, and he always invites others to faith and good deeds (Ma'ruf).

In narrations (Riwayat) and commentaries (Tafasir), several things have been mentioned as examples (Misdaq) of the good word (Kalimah Tayyibah): Monotheism (Tawhid), faith (Iman), correct beliefs, heavenly leaders, and their companions. Yes, Monotheism is a fixed root (Asl Thabit) that resides in the nature (Fitrah) and the depth of people's souls. «اصلها ثابت» (Its root is firm) and it affects all of man's actions, speech, and thoughts. «فرعها فى السماء» (Its branches reach the sky) and at all times, through every rise and fall, one can benefit from the fruit of faith. The tree of Monotheism has a fixed root. Threats, conspiracies, stings (hardships), sweets (temptations), and tyrants (Taghut) cannot uproot it. If, in narrations, this tree has been interpreted as the Prophet and his Ahl al-Bayt علیهم السلام, it is precisely because the religion of Muhammad صلى الله عليه وآله and the path of his Ahl al-Bayt, despite all those enemies, is expanding and will one day become global.

Messages

1- Let us pay attention to the examples in the Quran. «اَلَم تَر»

2- The true belief is a good word (Kalimah Tayyibah) whose root is fixed in the heart of the believer. «اصلها ثابت» (Its root is firm). And it is among the favors of Allah that He preserves the believers upon this belief. «یثبّت اللَّه الّذین آمنوا بالقول الثّابت»(47)

3- The word of truth is lasting, strong, and enduring. «كلمة طیبة... اصلها ثابت»(47) (Ibrahim, 27.)

Ayah

تُؤْتِىٓ أُكُلَهَا كُلَّ حِينٍۭ بِإِذْنِ رَبِّهَا ۗ وَيَضْرِبُ ٱللَّهُ ٱلْأَمْثَالَ لِلنَّاسِ لَعَلَّهُمْ يَتَذَكَّرُونَ (۲٥)
It gives its fruit every season by the leave of its Lord. Allah draws these parables for mankind so that they may take admonition. (25)

Key Points

Except for Allah and faith in Him, everything is perishable. «كلّ شى ء هالك الا وجهه»(48) And that which is perishable cannot bear fruit forever. However, every deed that has the Divine color is eternal. «ما عندكم ینفد و ما عنداللَّه باق»(49) Yes, the Divine color is permanent, and other colors fade away. «و من أحسن من اللَّه صبغة»(50) The tree of faith always bears fruit, and the believer is mindful of Allah and seeks to fulfill his duty in all circumstances: in ease or hardship, in joy or sorrow, in poverty and wealth. When threatened by oppressors, he resists until death. «اِنّا الى ربّنا راغبون»(51) When propagating (the message), he expects nothing from others. «اِن أجرى الاّ على اللَّه»(52) When angry, he restrains himself for His pleasure. «والكاظمین الغیظ»(53) When marrying, he relies upon Him. «اِن یكونوا فقراء یغنهم اللَّه من فضله»(54) When worshipping and obeying, he intends closeness (to Allah). «اِنّ صلاتى و نسكى و محیاى و مماتى للَّه ربّ العالمین»(55) When poor, he does not approach the wealthy and is not given to flattery. «ربّ انّى لما انزلت الىّ من خیر فقیر»(56) When victorious or defeated on the battlefield, he is content because he has fulfilled his duty. «اِحدى الحسنیین»(57) Yes! Faith in Allah the Almighty is like a tree whose fruit satisfies man at every moment, in this world, in Barzakh (the intermediate realm), and on the Day of Judgment. «تؤتى اُكُلها كلّ حین» But wealth, status, children, and other blessings are like a tree that bears fruit for only a few days, and that fruit is very meager. Not to mention that sometimes this very wealth, status, and children bear no fruit at all and only become a means of spiritual torment for man. «انّما یرید اللَّه لیعذّبهم بها»(58)

48) Qasas, 88. 49) Nahl, 96. 50) Baqarah, 138. 51) Qalam, 32. 52) Yunus, 72. 53) Aal-i-Imran, 134. 54) Noor, 32. 55) An'am, 162. 56) Qasas, 24. 57) Tawbah, 52. 58) Tawbah, 55.

Messages

1- The tree of faith always bears fruit, and the believer has no autumn or decay. «اكلها كل حین»

2- Analogy and similitude are a Quranic method for drawing people's attention and reminding them. «لعلهم یتذكرون»

Ayah

وَمَثَلُ كَلِمَةٍ خَبِيثَةٍ كَشَجَرَةٍ خَبِيثَةٍ ٱجْتُثَّتْ مِن فَوْقِ ٱلْأَرْضِ مَا لَهَا مِن قَرَارٍ (۲٦)
And the parable of a bad word is that of a bad tree: uprooted from the ground, it has no stability. (26)

Key Points

The word "ijthithāth" (اجتثاث) means eradication, destruction, and uprooting. Corrupt and deviant beliefs, words, and people have neither roots, nor fruit, nor flowers, nor beauty and fragrance, nor permanence, nor shade, nor growth; rather, they are thorns in the path of others.

Messages

1- One of the methods of teaching and training is the method of comparison. "Kalimatin ṭayyibah... Kalimatin khabīthah" (كلمة طیّبة... كلمة خبیثة)

2- Polytheism (shirk) and everything that is besides Allah (دون اللَّه) lacks stability and permanence. "Mā lahā min qarār" (ما لها من قرار)

3- False beliefs, like weeds, do not have roots deep in the earth, because they lack a proper foundation and basis. "Ujthithat min fawqi al-arḍ" (اجتثت من فوق الارض)

Ayah

يُثَبِّتُ ٱللَّهُ ٱلَّذِينَ ءَامَنُوا۟ بِٱلْقَوْلِ ٱلثَّابِتِ فِى ٱلْحَيَوٰةِ ٱلدُّنْيَا وَفِى ٱلْـَٔاخِرَةِ ۖ وَيُضِلُّ ٱللَّهُ ٱلظَّـٰلِمِينَ ۚ وَيَفْعَلُ ٱللَّهُ مَا يَشَآءُ (۲۷)
Allah fortifies those who have faith with a constant creed in the life of this world and in the Hereafter, and Allah leads astray the wrongdoers, and Allah does whatever He wishes. (27)

Messages

1- Divine assistance depends on the path that man chooses. «یثبّت اللَّه الذین آمنوا»

2- If Allah's help were not present, man would falter and fall in the face of temptations, desires, and tyrants (Taghuts). «یثبّت اللَّه الذین آمنوا»

3- The people of faith are logically firm and steadfast, and they stand against materialistic schools of thought. «یثبّت اللَّه الذین آمنوا بالقول الثابت فى الحیوة الدّنیا»

4- If man believes, Allah safeguards him, but if he inclines toward the evil tree and the false school of thought, Allah abandons him. «یثبّت اللَّه الذین امنوا... یضلّ اللَّه الظالمین»

5- Accepting false schools of thought is oppression (zulm) against oneself and the true doctrine. «الظالمین»

6- Divine wrath is dependent on the path that man chooses. «یُضل اللَّه الظالمین»

Ayahs

۞ أَلَمْ تَرَ إِلَى ٱلَّذِينَ بَدَّلُوا۟ نِعْمَتَ ٱللَّهِ كُفْرًا وَأَحَلُّوا۟ قَوْمَهُمْ دَارَ ٱلْبَوَارِ (۲۸)
Have you not regarded those who have changed Allah’s blessing with ingratitude, and landed their people in the house of ruin? (28)
جَهَنَّمَ يَصْلَوْنَهَا ۖ وَبِئْسَ ٱلْقَرَارُ (۲۹)
—hell, which they shall enter, and it is an evil abode! (29)

Key Points

Bawār (بَوار) means severe stagnation and depression that leads to destruction/perdition. (59) The disbelievers and polytheists transformed the great divine blessings into disbelief (kufr): A: Instead of the blessing of Monotheism (Tawhid), they chose polytheism (Shirk). B: They abandoned the blessing of pure nature (fitrah) and resorted to imitating their misguided ancestors. C: They preferred superstitions over divine revelation. D: They showed ingratitude towards the blessing of heavenly leaders and followed the tyrants (Taghuts).

Regarding "بدّلوا نعمت اللَّه" (They exchanged the favor of Allah), we read in many narrations that the Shi'a Imams, علیهم السلام, said: "نحن واللَّه نعمة اللَّه التى انعم بها على عباده" (By Allah, we are the favor of Allah which He bestowed upon His servants). (60) By Allah, the blessings that were exchanged were our very existence. People abandoned us and went after other leaders. The phrase "بدّلوا نعمت اللَّه" (They exchanged the favor of Allah) is similar to what is mentioned in other verses. For example, in Ayah 41 of Surah Al-Qasas, He says: "و جعلناهم ائمة یدعون الى النّار" (And We made them leaders inviting to the Fire), and in Ayah 98 of Surah Al-Kahf, He says: "یقدم قومه یوم القیامة فَأوردهم النّار" (He will precede his people on the Day of Resurrection and lead them into the Fire).

59) Mufradāt al-Rāghib. 60) Majma‘ al-Bayān.

Messages

1- Any exchange, alteration, or distortion of divine blessings and signs is disbelief (kufr). "بدلوا...كفراً" (They exchanged... for disbelief).

2- Deviant leaders become the cause of destruction and corruption in society and send people to Hell. "واحلّوا قومهم دار البوار" (And they lodged their people in the abode of perdition).

Ayah

وَجَعَلُوا۟ لِلَّهِ أَندَادًا لِّيُضِلُّوا۟ عَن سَبِيلِهِۦ ۗ قُلْ تَمَتَّعُوا۟ فَإِنَّ مَصِيرَكُمْ إِلَى ٱلنَّارِ (۳٠)
They have set up equals to Allah, to lead [people] astray from His way. Say, ‘Enjoy [for a while], for indeed your destination is hellfire!’ (30)

Key Points

Some ascribe partners to Allah in the creation of the world, and some imagine partners for God in His blessings. They consider possessing health, knowledge, power, honor, and wealth to be due to their own skill or the power of others, but they very soon realize that they have gone astray.

Messages

1- Among the worst types of ingratitude for blessings is setting up partners for Allah and misleading people. «و جعلوا للَّه انداداً لیضلّوا»

2- Sometimes the source of polytheism is the tyrants (Taghut). «و جعلوا للَّه انداداً لیضلّوا» And sometimes the allure of idols is the cause of deviation. «واجنُبنى و بنّى ان نعبد الاصنام ربّ انهنّ اضللن كثیراً من النّاس»(61)

3- Allah leaves some individuals to their own devices. «قل تمتّعوا»

4- Disbelievers enjoy success in this world, but they have no Hereafter. «تمتعوا فانّ مصیركم الى النّار»

5- Not every comfort is a sign of affection; perhaps it is a means and prelude to Divine wrath. «تمتّعوا» (61) Ibrahim, 35 - 36.

Ayah

قُل لِّعِبَادِىَ ٱلَّذِينَ ءَامَنُوا۟ يُقِيمُوا۟ ٱلصَّلَوٰةَ وَيُنفِقُوا۟ مِمَّا رَزَقْنَـٰهُمْ سِرًّا وَعَلَانِيَةً مِّن قَبْلِ أَن يَأْتِىَ يَوْمٌ لَّا بَيْعٌ فِيهِ وَلَا خِلَـٰلٌ (۳۱)
Tell My servants who have faith to maintain the prayer and to spend out of what We have provided them with, secretly and openly, before there comes a day on which there will be neither any bargaining nor friendship. (31)

Key Points

Open charity (infaq) is a means of educating the next generation, encouraging others, and clearing oneself of suspicion, while hidden charity is a factor for growth and sincerity (ikhlas). Of course, some commentators have said that open charity relates to obligatory charities, such as Khums and Zakat, and hidden charity relates to recommended (mustahabb) cases, such as Sadaqah (voluntary alms). Although both open and hidden charity have effects, perhaps it can be said that hidden charity is better, because in this Ayah the word “sirran” (secretly) precedes “‘alāniyatan” (openly).

Messages

1- When giving a command, grant status to the recipient. (li-‘ibādī - "to My servants"). Servitude to Allah is a medal of honor for the believers.

2- Faith of the heart is not enough; prayer (Salat), charity (infaq), and righteous deeds are necessary. (āmanū yuqīmū al-ṣalāta wa yunfiqū).

3- Islam is a comprehensive religion. The relationship with Allah and helping the deprived are side-by-side and are conditions for the acceptance of one another. (yuqīmū al-ṣalāta wa yunfiqū).

4- The subject of charity is not only wealth; we must give charity from everything we possess (whether knowledge, wealth, reputation, or power). (mimmā razaqnāhum - "from what We have provided them").

5- Charity must be from the provision of Allah, meaning lawful (halal) wealth, not from just any wealth we possess. He said: (razaqnāhum - "We have provided them") and did not say (‘indakum - "you possess").

6- Charity must sometimes be hidden and sometimes open. (sirran wa ‘alāniyatan).

7- Utilize opportunities. (min qabli an ya’tiya yawm... - "before there comes a Day...").

8- If you seek trade, deal with Allah in this world, because there will be no transaction in the Hereafter. (yawmun lā bay‘un fīhi - "a Day wherein there is no selling/trade").

9- One cannot escape the punishment of the Day of Judgment through money or connections. (lā bay‘un fīhi wa lā khilāl - "no selling/trade therein and no friendship/intimacy").

Ayah

ٱللَّهُ ٱلَّذِى خَلَقَ ٱلسَّمَـٰوَٰتِ وَٱلْأَرْضَ وَأَنزَلَ مِنَ ٱلسَّمَآءِ مَآءً فَأَخْرَجَ بِهِۦ مِنَ ٱلثَّمَرَٰتِ رِزْقًا لَّكُمْ ۖ وَسَخَّرَ لَكُمُ ٱلْفُلْكَ لِتَجْرِىَ فِى ٱلْبَحْرِ بِأَمْرِهِۦ ۖ وَسَخَّرَ لَكُمُ ٱلْأَنْهَـٰرَ (۳۲)
It is Allah who created the heavens and the earth, and He sends down water from the sky and with it He brings forth crops for your sustenance. And He disposed the ships for you[r benefit] so that they may sail at sea by His command, and He disposed the rivers for you. (32)

Key Points

This Ayah refers to the issue of water from three angles: A: Rainwater, which is the means of irrigating plants, the source of their life, and the cause of purifying and changing and refining the air. B: Seawater, which sustains the life of marine animals, is the best and cheapest route for the passage of ships, and is the source of clouds and rain. C: River water, which is the means of supplying water to arid regions. In the previous Ayah, Allah commanded prayer (Salat) and spending (Infaq). In this Ayah, He says: Why are you negligent in spending, when Allah has given you everything? Tas-khīr (subjugation/making subservient) means both utilizing benefits, such as the subjugation of the sun and the moon, and also the complete dominance and authority of man, such as the subjugation of ships and rivers and man's control over them.

Messages

1- Recognizing Allah's blessings is the best way to know Him (God-knowledge), which is accompanied by general understanding, love, motivation, and worship. «خلق السموات... سخّر لكم»

2- Among all of Allah's blessings, water is one of the most important. «ماءً - فى البحر انهار»

3- The creation of existence is for humanity. «رزقا لكم - سخّر لكم»

4- Although material means play a role, all factors are by the command of Allah. «بامره»

5- Man is dominant over nature. «سخّر لكم»

Ayah

وَسَخَّرَ لَكُمُ ٱلشَّمْسَ وَٱلْقَمَرَ دَآئِبَيْنِ ۖ وَسَخَّرَ لَكُمُ ٱلَّيْلَ وَٱلنَّهَارَ (۳۳)
He disposed the sun and the moon for you, constant [in their courses], and He disposed the night and the day, (33)

Key Points

Dā'ibīn (دائبین) is derived from da'b (دأب), meaning continuous habit or custom. For example: كدأب آل فرعون (like the custom of Pharaoh's people). Al-Maraghi (مراغى) stated in his commentary (Tafsir) that Dā'ibīn means "those constantly in motion" (دائمین فى الحركة), like: كلّ فى فلك یسبحون (each [celestial body] swims in an orbit). Yes, the sun is a great power station that works constantly, unlike human power stations which are shut down and repaired every so often. What would happen if the sun were absent for a period? Where would the lives of humans, plants, and animals end up? The existence of numerous microbes and the absence of heat and vapor, etc., would face the world with what calamity?

The cloud, the wind, the moon, the sun, and the heavens are all working So that you may obtain a loaf of bread and not eat it heedlessly. All are bewildered and obedient for your sake; It would not be fair if you were not obedient [to God].

Messages

1- Not only the earth and its earthly blessings, but also the heavens and the celestial bodies have been created for the benefit of mankind and according to his needs. سخر لكم الشّمس و القمر (He subjected the sun and the moon for you).

Ayah

وَءَاتَىٰكُم مِّن كُلِّ مَا سَأَلْتُمُوهُ ۚ وَإِن تَعُدُّوا۟ نِعْمَتَ ٱللَّهِ لَا تُحْصُوهَآ ۗ إِنَّ ٱلْإِنسَـٰنَ لَظَلُومٌ كَفَّارٌ (۳٤)
and He gave you all that you had asked Him. If you enumerate Allah’s blessings, you will not be able to count them. Indeed man is most unfair and ungrateful! (34)

Key Points

In Ayah 18 of Surah An-Nahl, we read: «ان تعدوا نعمت اللَّه لاتحصوها انّ اللَّه لغفور رحیم» (If you attempt to count the favors of Allah, you cannot enumerate them. Indeed, Allah is Forgiving and Merciful.) If you want to count the blessings of God, you cannot. Indeed, Allah is Forgiving and Merciful. In the preceding verses, we read that corrupt leaders invite people toward others besides Allah. «جعلوا للَّه اندادا» (They set up rivals to Allah.) This verse states: Although nothing can be accomplished by anyone other than Allah, and everything anyone possesses belongs to Allah, man is heedless and ungrateful. 'Add' (عدّ) means counting, 'Ihsā' (احصاء) means counting with precision in such a way that nothing is overlooked.

Messages

1- The factors of production are at man's disposal (water, soil, sunlight...). If there is inadequacy or shortage, it is due to mismanagement and improper distribution. «و آتیكم من كلّ... ان الانسان لظلوم كفّار»

2- The God who created mankind fulfilled all his needs through the proper means, but man seeks to fulfill his needs through sin. «اتاكم من كل ما سالتموه... ان الانسان لظلوم كفار»

3- Man is a needy and dependent being. «سألتموه»

4- Man does not have the power to count the blessings of Allah, let alone render thanks for them. «لاتحصوها»

5- If man does not utilize the divine blessings properly, he is extremely unjust and ungrateful. «لظلوم كفّار»

Ayah

وَإِذْ قَالَ إِبْرَٰهِيمُ رَبِّ ٱجْعَلْ هَـٰذَا ٱلْبَلَدَ ءَامِنًا وَٱجْنُبْنِى وَبَنِىَّ أَن نَّعْبُدَ ٱلْأَصْنَامَ (۳٥)
When Abraham said, ‘My Lord! Make this city a sanctuary, and save me and my children from worshiping idols. (35)

Key Points

In this Ayah and the subsequent Ayahs, a portrait of the supplications of Prophet Ibrahim (Abraham) is presented, which demonstrates his fervor and great spirit. Perhaps it is because of these very supplications that this Surah was named "Ibrahim."

Ibrahim عليه السلام supplicated for Mecca at two times: The first time was when he settled Isma'il (Ishmael) and Hajar (Hagar) there, saying: «ربّ اجعل هذا بلداً آمناً» (62) "O Lord! Make this place a secure city." The second time was when a population came to Mecca and settled in the city. Here too, Ibrahim supplicated, saying: «ربّ اجعل هذا البلد آمنا»

Question: Did all individuals of Ibrahim's progeny become monotheists because of this supplication? Answer: Supplication is one factor, and the will of the children is another factor. Let us not forget the story of Noah's son.

Question: How did Ibrahim عليه السلام refer to "Mecca" as a city, even though in the subsequent Ayahs He says: "This is a land unfit for cultivation"? Answer: Mecca being a city was either after the arrival of tribes in this region (and this supplication relates to that time), or we can say that there is no contradiction between a region being a city and being unfit for cultivation. Even now, Mecca is a city unfit for cultivation.

Question: Although Prophet Ibrahim عليه السلام was the champion of Monotheism (Tawhid), why did he supplicate, "O God! Keep me far from polytheism (Shirk)"? Answer: The Prophet of Islam صلى الله عليه وآله also always walked the Straight Path, yet in every prayer, he would say: «اهدنا الصّراط المستقیم» (Guide us to the Straight Path). This means that even if someone is certain that he is walking the right path, he must still fear the danger of deviation and seek help from Allah.

The intent of "establishing security" is a law that guarantees the security of Mecca, not that Mecca has been secure throughout history. This is because the Ka'bah was attacked by enemies many times, blood was spilled in Mecca, the Prophet of Islam صلى الله عليه وآله and his companions were tortured, and Imam Husayn عليه السلام abandoned the Hajj due to the insecurity of Mecca. However, the Divine Law has designated that place as a secure region.

62) بقره، ۱۲۶.

Messages

1- Supplication for the preservation of correct thought and belief is among the best supplications. «واجنبنى و بنى ان نعبد الاصنام»

2- Centers of worship must possess security. «هذا البلد آمنا»

3- Security is a necessary condition for life. «البلد آمنا»

4- Leaders of society must be concerned with the security of the people. «ربّ اجعل... آمنا»

5- Security is not for the freedom to sin and indulge in luxury; rather, security must be a prelude to worship. «امناً» «اَمناً یعبدوننى» (63)

6- The possibility of the danger of polytheism (Shirk) is not far from any station, even the champion of Monotheism, Ibrahim. «و اجنبنى و بنّى...»

7- In supplications, we should also remember others, especially our children. «واجنبنى و بنّى»

63) نور، ۵۵.

Ayah

رَبِّ إِنَّهُنَّ أَضْلَلْنَ كَثِيرًا مِّنَ ٱلنَّاسِ ۖ فَمَن تَبِعَنِى فَإِنَّهُۥ مِنِّى ۖ وَمَنْ عَصَانِى فَإِنَّكَ غَفُورٌ رَّحِيمٌ (۳٦)
My Lord! Indeed they have misled many people. So whoever follows me indeed belongs to me, and as for those who disobey me, well, You are indeed all-forgiving, all-merciful. (36)

Key Points

| Question: How do idols, which are statues made of stone and wood, mislead humans? Answer: Firstly: Idols are not always inanimate objects; sometimes humans and tyrants (Taghuts) also become idols. Secondly: The issue of art and the ornaments accompanying the idols had an attraction. Thirdly: Sometimes ignorance causes even stone and wood to gain value. If we interpret this Ayah alongside the previous Ayah, we achieve a good result, because in the previous Ayah, Abraham said: "O Allah! Keep my children away from idol worship." In this Ayah, [Abraham] says: "Whoever was a monotheist and followed me is of me." Therefore, all monotheists are the ideological children of Abraham. "The religion of your father Abraham" (ملّة ابیكم ابراهیم). Just as the Messenger of Allah صلى الله علیه وآله said: «اَنَا و علّى ابوا هذه الاُمّة»(64) I and Ali are the ideological fathers of this Ummah. But if a polytheist is even the son of the Prophet, he is addressed: «انّه لیس من اهلك»(65) Imam Ali عليه السلام said: «انّ ولىّ محمّد من اطاع اللَّه و ان بعدت لحمته و انّ عدوّ محمّد من عصى اللَّه و ان قربت لحمته» "Indeed, the friend of Muhammad is he who obeys Allah, even if his kinship is distant, and the enemy of Muhammad is he who disobeys Allah, even if his kinship is close."(66)

64) بحار، ج 16، 95. 65) هود، 46. 66) بحار، ج 67، ص 25.

Messages

1- Arts like sculpture can become a source of deviation, just as idols misled many people. «انّهنّ اضللن»

2- Piety is not achieved through slogans; it is achieved through action and following commands. «تبعنى»

3- An ideological relationship exists between the heavenly leaders and the believing people. «فانّه منّى»

4- Prophets were compassionate even towards their opponents and did not cause them despair. «مَن عصانى فانّك غفور رحیم»

5- Opposition to the Prophet's command is opposition to Allah. «من عصانى فانّك»

Ayah

رَّبَّنَآ إِنِّىٓ أَسْكَنتُ مِن ذُرِّيَّتِى بِوَادٍ غَيْرِ ذِى زَرْعٍ عِندَ بَيْتِكَ ٱلْمُحَرَّمِ رَبَّنَا لِيُقِيمُوا۟ ٱلصَّلَوٰةَ فَٱجْعَلْ أَفْـِٔدَةً مِّنَ ٱلنَّاسِ تَهْوِىٓ إِلَيْهِمْ وَٱرْزُقْهُم مِّنَ ٱلثَّمَرَٰتِ لَعَلَّهُمْ يَشْكُرُونَ (۳۷)
Our Lord! I have settled part of my descendants in a barren valley, by Your sacred House, our Lord, that they may maintain the prayer. So make the hearts of a part of the people fond of them, and provide them with fruits, so that they may give thanks. (37)

Key Points

When Allah granted Isma'il (Ishmael) to Abraham (Ibrahim) in his old age, He commanded him to settle this child and his mother in Mecca. Abraham obeyed the Divine command and then prayed for them. We read in narrations that Imam Baqir عليه السلام said: We, the Household of the Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله, are the remainder of the progeny of Abraham, and the hearts of the people incline towards us. He then recited this verse: «ربّنا انى اسكنت ... فاجعل افئدة من النّاس»

The Ka'bah was placed in a barren, waterless region so that people might be tested by it. As Imam Ali عليه السلام stated in the Qasi'ah Sermon: If the Ka'bah had been in a place with pleasant water and climate, people would not have visited it for the sake of Allah.

The prayers of the Prophets are answered. «و ارزقهم من الّثمرات» (and provide them with fruits), «حَرَماً آمناً یجبى الیه ثمرات كل شى ء»(67) (a secure sanctuary to which the fruits of all things are brought). A secure sanctuary where all kinds of fruits are provided. Imam Baqir عليه السلام said: Every fruit that exists in the East and West of the world is found in Mecca.

67) Al-Qasas (The Stories), 57.

Messages

1- The choice of residence for the Prophets was based on prayer and worship. «اسكنت - لیقیموا الصلوة»

2- Piety is sometimes accompanied by displacement, migration, separation from family, and deprivation of welfare facilities. «اسكنت من ذریّتى بواد غیر زرع»

3- The Ka'bah and its surroundings were respected and subject to restrictions and prohibitions even during the time of Prophet Abraham. «بیتك المحرّم»

4- The axis and purpose of Abraham's movement was prayer. «ربّنا لیقیموا الصلوة»

5- Those who perform prayer should be beloved. «لیقیموالصلوة فاجعل افئدة من الناس»

6- Prayer existed in previous religions as well. «لیقیمواالصلوة»

7- Popularity and the inclination of hearts are in the hands of Allah. «فاجعل افئدة...»

8- Not all people are worthy of loving the friends of Allah. «من الناس»

9- Men of Allah desire the world for a lofty goal. «من الثمرات لعلّهم یشكرون»

Ayah

رَبَّنَآ إِنَّكَ تَعْلَمُ مَا نُخْفِى وَمَا نُعْلِنُ ۗ وَمَا يَخْفَىٰ عَلَى ٱللَّهِ مِن شَىْءٍ فِى ٱلْأَرْضِ وَلَا فِى ٱلسَّمَآءِ (۳۸)
Our Lord! Indeed You know whatever we hide and whatever we disclose, and nothing is hidden from Allah on the earth or in the sky. (38)

Key Points

Now that everything is clear to Allah, and His knowledge encompasses everything and all our actions, we must not commit sin in His presence, nor should we dispose of His creations except according to His pleasure and commands.

Messages

1- Hidden and manifest have no meaning before Allah. «تعلم ما نخفى و ما نعلن»

2- The earth and the heaven are equal before the knowledge of Allah. «ما یخفى... فى الارض ولا فى السماء»

Ayah

ٱلْحَمْدُ لِلَّهِ ٱلَّذِى وَهَبَ لِى عَلَى ٱلْكِبَرِ إِسْمَـٰعِيلَ وَإِسْحَـٰقَ ۚ إِنَّ رَبِّى لَسَمِيعُ ٱلدُّعَآءِ (۳۹)
All praise belongs to Allah, who gave me Ishmael and Isaac despite [my] old age. Indeed my Lord hears all supplications. (39)

Key Points

Ishmael and Isaac are among the special blessings of Allah because: 1. They came into existence through the prayer of the Prophet of Allah, Abraham. 2. They were born during the old age of their father. 3. They were righteous offspring. 4. They became the progenitors of subsequent prophets. Moreover, what is important is the righteous offspring, whether they are born of a slave-woman or otherwise. (Ishmael was from a slave-woman and Isaac was from a free woman.)

Messages

1- When mentioning divine blessings, we should thank Allah. «الحمدللَّه»

2- We should thank God for the prayers that have been answered. «انّ ربّى لسمیع الدعاء»

3- A child is a divine gift. «وَهَب لى»

4- There is no obstacle to the divine Will and power. «على الكبر» Old age is not a barrier to having children.

5- When thanking Allah, mention the blessings separately. «اسماعیل و اسحاق»

6- The answering of prayer is the tradition (Sunnah) of Allah. «انّ ربّى لسمیع الدّعاء»

Ayahs

رَبِّ ٱجْعَلْنِى مُقِيمَ ٱلصَّلَوٰةِ وَمِن ذُرِّيَّتِى ۚ رَبَّنَا وَتَقَبَّلْ دُعَآءِ (٤٠)
My Lord! Make me a maintainer of prayer, and my descendants [as well]. Our Lord, accept my supplication. (40)
رَبَّنَا ٱغْفِرْ لِى وَلِوَٰلِدَىَّ وَلِلْمُؤْمِنِينَ يَوْمَ يَقُومُ ٱلْحِسَابُ (٤۱)
Our Lord! Forgive me and my parents, and all the faithful, on the day when the reckoning is held.’ (41)

Key Points

In the verses we have read so far concerning Prophet Ibrahim (Abraham) in this Surah, he makes seven requests to Allah: the security of Mecca, keeping away from idol worship, the turning of the hearts and thoughts of the believers towards his children and his school of thought, the benefiting of his progeny from the fruits (of the land), the success in establishing prayer (Salat), the acceptance of his supplications, and forgiveness for himself, his parents (walidayn), and all believers. The term walid (والد) is used only for the biological father, but ab (أب) is used for others besides the father, including the paternal uncle and the father-in-law. Since Ibrahim's parents (walidayn) were believers, Prophet Ibrahim prays for his parents in this verse. However, in other verses where ab (أب) is used, referring to Ibrahim's paternal uncle, the Prophet dissociates himself from him because he was a polytheist (mushrik). We also read in Ayah 133 of Surah Al-Baqarah that the sons of Ya'qub (Jacob), in response to their father who had asked: «ما تعبدون من بعدى» (What will you worship after me?), replied: «نعبد الهك و اله آبائك ابراهیم و اسماعیل» (We worship your God and the God of your fathers, Ibrahim and Isma'il). Although Isma'il was Ya'qub's paternal uncle, the term ab (أب) was used for the uncle.

Messages

  1. The repetition of Rabb (Lord) at the beginning of Prophet Ibrahim's supplications is a sign of its effectiveness in the acceptance of the prayer, or it is one of the etiquettes of supplication. «ربّ اجعلنى»
  2. Seek help from Allah for the establishment of prayer for yourself and your children. «ربّ اجعلنى»
  3. Prayer (Salat) is the focus of Prophet Ibrahim's supplications. «ربّنا لیقیموا الصّلاة», « ربّ اجعلنى مقیم الصّلاة»
  4. Although the mission of the Prophets is the establishment of religion (Din), praying for the establishment of prayer instead (of praying for the establishment of the entire Din) indicates that prayer is the complete face and manifestation of religion. «مقیم الصّلاة»
  5. The establishment of prayer is the equivalent and counterpart of leadership (Imamate). Prophet Ibrahim used the phrase «و من ذریّتى» (and from my progeny) in two instances: one concerning the prayer of his descendants «مقیم الصّلاة و من ذریّتى», and one concerning the leadership of his descendants when Allah said to Ibrahim: «انى جاعلك للنّاس اماما قال و من ذریّتى»(68) (Indeed, I will make you an Imam for mankind. He said: And [make Imams] from my progeny?). Ibrahim said: My Lord, grant my progeny this status as well.
  6. In supplication, pay attention both to yourself, «واجنبنى - واجعلنى - واغفرلى» (keep me away - make me - forgive me), and to others. «وبنّى - ومن ذریّتى - و لوالدىّ وللمؤمنین» (and my sons - and from my progeny - and for my parents and for the believers).
  7. Man must think both of the preceding generation, «ولوالدىّ وللمؤمنین » (and for my parents and for the believers), and of the succeeding generation. «و من ذریّتى»
  8. In supplication, think of the Day of Judgment for yourself, your children, and society. «یوم یقوم الحساب»(68) (Al-Baqarah, 124).

Ayah

وَلَا تَحْسَبَنَّ ٱللَّهَ غَـٰفِلًا عَمَّا يَعْمَلُ ٱلظَّـٰلِمُونَ ۚ إِنَّمَا يُؤَخِّرُهُمْ لِيَوْمٍ تَشْخَصُ فِيهِ ٱلْأَبْصَـٰرُ (٤۲)
Do not suppose that Allah is oblivious of what the wrongdoers are doing. He is only granting them respite until the day when the eyes will be glazed. (42)

Key Points

| Question: Why does Allah, who is Just and Powerful, not stop the oppressors? Answer: Allah is both Just and Capable, and He is aware of the actions of the oppressors, but the delay in retribution is because the world does not have the capacity for complete reward or punishment. It is the Sunnah (tradition) of Allah to grant His servants respite so that they may repent if they have the capacity, and if they are not capable, He will settle their account on the Day of Judgment.

Messages

1- Do not judge quickly and do not assume Allah is unaware. «لاتحسبنّ»

2- Granting respite to the oppressors is not a sign of Allah's heedlessness or the Lord's satisfaction with them. «و لا تحسبنّ اللَّه غافلا»

3- It is the Sunnah of Allah to grant respite. «انّما یؤخرهم»

Therefore, do not remain silent or despair in the face of the oppressor; fulfill your duty, for Allah is lying in wait for them.

4- Divine retribution and reward have a timeline. «لیومٍ»

5- The Day of Judgment is so terrifying and dreadful that eyes will stare fixedly. «تشخص فیه الابصار»

Ayah

مُهْطِعِينَ مُقْنِعِى رُءُوسِهِمْ لَا يَرْتَدُّ إِلَيْهِمْ طَرْفُهُمْ ۖ وَأَفْـِٔدَتُهُمْ هَوَآءٌ (٤۳)
Scrambling with their heads upturned, there will be a fixed gaze in their eyes, and their hearts will be vacant. (43)

Key Points

For the word muhṭi'īn (مهطعین), which is derived from ihṭā' (اهطاء), several meanings have been given: craning the neck, rushing (speeding up), and looking with humiliation. All these meanings can be intended in this Ayah.

Ayah

وَأَنذِرِ ٱلنَّاسَ يَوْمَ يَأْتِيهِمُ ٱلْعَذَابُ فَيَقُولُ ٱلَّذِينَ ظَلَمُوا۟ رَبَّنَآ أَخِّرْنَآ إِلَىٰٓ أَجَلٍ قَرِيبٍ نُّجِبْ دَعْوَتَكَ وَنَتَّبِعِ ٱلرُّسُلَ ۗ أَوَلَمْ تَكُونُوٓا۟ أَقْسَمْتُم مِّن قَبْلُ مَا لَكُم مِّن زَوَالٍ (٤٤)
Warn the people of the day when the punishment will overtake them, whereat the wrongdoers will say, ‘Our Lord! Respite us for a short time so that we may respond to Your call and follow the apostles.’ [They will be told,] ‘Did you not use to swear earlier that there would be no reverse for you, (44)

Key Points

Considering the previous Ayah, it initially seems that this Ayah is also about the Day of Judgment (Qiyamah). However, the phrase «اخّرنا الى اجل قریب نُجب دعوتك ...» (If You would delay us for a short term, we will answer Your call...) suggests more strongly that the Ayah is about divine punishment in this world, because the delay of punishment, answering Allah's call, and following the prophets relate to this world, not the Day of Judgment. Requests to return to the world also exist on the Day of Judgment, and verses regarding this have repeatedly appeared in the Quran, including:

A: «فهل الى خروج من سبیل»(69) Is there any way out of destruction? B: «فارجعنا نعمل صالحا»(70) Return us to the world so that we may perform righteous deeds. C: «ربّنا اخرجنا نعمل صالحا»(71) Our Lord! Take us out of the Fire so that we may perform good deeds.

(69) Ghafir, 11. (70) Sajdah, 12. (71) Fatir, 37.

Messages

1- Warning and threat are among the methods and ways of educating and guiding the misguided. «وانذر»

2- The warnings of the Quran pertain to all people. «انذر الناس»

3- Criminals will regret one day, but lost opportunities will not return. «اخرّنا...»

4- When divine punishment descends, the path of escape and repentance is closed. «اولم تكونوا...»

Ayah

وَسَكَنتُمْ فِى مَسَـٰكِنِ ٱلَّذِينَ ظَلَمُوٓا۟ أَنفُسَهُمْ وَتَبَيَّنَ لَكُمْ كَيْفَ فَعَلْنَا بِهِمْ وَضَرَبْنَا لَكُمُ ٱلْأَمْثَالَ (٤٥)
while you dwelt in the dwellings of those who had wronged themselves [before], and it had been made clear to you how We had dealt with them [before you], and We had [also] cited examples for you?’ (45)

Messages

1- Many humans do not take heed from the fate of their predecessors, and despite the fact that they are dwelling in their places, they forget everything. «سكنتم فى مساكن الذین ظلموا»

2- Allah completes the argument (establishes proof). «الذین ظلموا انفسهم، ضربنا لكم الامثال» (He both describes the punishment of previous nations and clarifies matters through multiple examples.)

3- The traditions (Sunan) of Allah are fixed and govern history and society. «تبین لكم كیف فعلنا بهم»

Ayah

وَقَدْ مَكَرُوا۟ مَكْرَهُمْ وَعِندَ ٱللَّهِ مَكْرُهُمْ وَإِن كَانَ مَكْرُهُمْ لِتَزُولَ مِنْهُ ٱلْجِبَالُ (٤٦)
They certainly devised their plots, but their plots are known to Allah, and their plots are not such as to dislodge the mountains. (46)

Key Points

Allah is both aware of their deceit and has complete mastery over it, and He will punish all those conspiracies, and He can repel and neutralize their plot, or turn it back upon themselves. It is possible that the intent of «جبال» (mountains) is the religion of Islam and the Prophet, who stands firm and steadfast like a mountain against the deceit and trickery of the enemies.

Messages

1- Oppressors, even if they possess power and strength, will ultimately be disgraced by Allah. «عنداللَّه مكرهم»

Ayah
