فَلَا تَحْسَبَنَّ ٱللَّهَ مُخْلِفَ وَعْدِهِۦ رُسُلَهُۥٓ ۗ إِنَّ ٱللَّهَ عَزِيزٌ ذُو ٱنتِقَامٍ (٤۷)
So do not suppose that Allah will break His promise to His apostles. Indeed Allah is all-mighty, avenger. (47)

Key Points

Human vengeance is accompanied by malice and the satisfaction of the heart, but Divine vengeance is based on justice, wisdom, and discipline (or correction).

Messages

1- Necessary warnings must be given during moments of danger and error. «لاتحسبنّ»

2- Delay in Divine assistance should not cause you doubt. «لا تحسبنّ»

3- Although man possesses intellect and innate nature (fitrah) and seeks counsel, if there is no revelation (Wahy), he will fall into deviation in understanding and perception. «لاتحسبنّ»

4- The respite that Allah grants to the disbelievers and oppressors is based on a tradition (Sunnah) and wisdom, not out of negligence or breaking His promises. «فلا تحسبنّ اللَّه مخلف وعده»

5- Why would the God who possesses power break His promise? «انّ اللَّه عزیز ذو انتقام»

Ayah

يَوْمَ تُبَدَّلُ ٱلْأَرْضُ غَيْرَ ٱلْأَرْضِ وَٱلسَّمَـٰوَٰتُ ۖ وَبَرَزُوا۟ لِلَّهِ ٱلْوَٰحِدِ ٱلْقَهَّارِ (٤۸)
The day when the earth is turned into another earth and the heavens [as well], and they are presented before Allah, the One, the All-paramount (48)

Key Points

The transformation and change of the earth on the Day of Resurrection (Qiyamah) have been mentioned in numerous verses. On that Day, a severe earthquake will occur, mountains will be uprooted from the earth, set into motion, and become soft like wool and cotton. (72) It is a Day when the earth will become a flat, level, and smooth expanse. As the Quran states: «یسئلونك عن الجبال فقل ینسفها ربّى نسفاً فیذرها قاعا صفصفا» (73) However, the transformation of the heavens occurs through the extinguishing of the sun and the folding up of the skies. In any case, this existing system has an end.

72) Al-Zalzalah, 1. 73) Taha, 105-106.

Messages

1- All creatures will be resurrected again for the reckoning of their deeds. «برزوا»

2- On the Day of Resurrection, there is no place for mercy or compassion toward the oppressors. «القهار»

Ayahs

وَتَرَى ٱلْمُجْرِمِينَ يَوْمَئِذٍ مُّقَرَّنِينَ فِى ٱلْأَصْفَادِ (٤۹)
—on that day you will see the guilty bound together in chains, (49)
سَرَابِيلُهُم مِّن قَطِرَانٍ وَتَغْشَىٰ وُجُوهَهُمُ ٱلنَّارُ (٥٠)
their garments made of pitch, and the Fire covering their faces, (50)

Key Points

A person suffering pain and torment is afflicted more if he hears the cries of others, just as happiness is pleasurable, and being among a joyful group is even more pleasurable. For this reason, the Quran states: We gather people of the same thought and path in one place, the criminals in Hell and the righteous in Paradise. «والّذین آمنوا ... الحقنا بهم ذریّتهم»(74) And concerning the people of Hell, we read: «احشروا الّذین ظلموا و ازواجهم»(75) The oppressors and their spouses who were like them will go to Hell together. Elsewhere we read: «فكبكبوا فیها هم و الغاوون»(76) On that Day, the worshipper, along with his imaginary object of worship, will be cast into Hell together, and perhaps the Ayah «و اذا النّفوس زُوّجت»(77) also expresses this pairing. (And Allah knows best).

«اصفاد» is the plural of «صفود», which refers to a 'kunda' (stocks/shackles), a large piece of wood tied to the feet of prisoners.

«قطران» (tar/pitch) is a substance extracted from certain trees, which, after boiling and solidifying, is rubbed onto the body of a camel so that the severe burning sensation helps cure the camel's skin diseases. This substance is foul-smelling and flammable.(78)

One of the torments of the Day of Judgment is the binding of criminals with chains and fetters, and this meaning is mentioned in several Ayahs. Including: «و ترى المجرمین ... فى الاصفاد» and «خذوه فغلّوه»(79) and «اذ الاغلال فى اعناقهم»(80). The criminals will be chained together with other criminals, or with the devils. Or their hands will be bound with fetters, or their hands and feet, or their hands, feet, and necks will be constrained together. «مقرّنین»

74) At-Tur, 21. 75) As-Saffat, 23. 76) Ash-Shu'ara, 94. 77) At-Takwir, 7. 78) Tafsir-e Namuneh. 79) Al-Haqqah, 30. 80) Ghafir, 71.

Messages

1- The Quran depicts the Day of Judgment in such a way that it is as if we are seeing it now. «ترى»

2- The humiliation and torment of the criminals will be in public. «وترى المجرمین»

3- If we look through the lens of the embodiment of deeds (Tajassum al-A'mal), we might conclude that clothes worn with coquetry, pride, extravagance, and lustfulness will be transformed into the black, foul-smelling, and flammable garments of the Day of Judgment. «سرابيلهم من قطران»

Ayah

لِيَجْزِىَ ٱللَّهُ كُلَّ نَفْسٍ مَّا كَسَبَتْ ۚ إِنَّ ٱللَّهَ سَرِيعُ ٱلْحِسَابِ (٥۱)
so that Allah may reward every soul for what it has earned. Indeed Allah is swift at reckoning. (51)

Messages

1- None of the people will remain without recompense. «كل نفس»

2- No deed in this world is without a response. «ما كسبت»

3- Divine punishment and reward are based on our own actions. «ما كسبت»

Ayah

هَـٰذَا بَلَـٰغٌ لِّلنَّاسِ وَلِيُنذَرُوا۟ بِهِۦ وَلِيَعْلَمُوٓا۟ أَنَّمَا هُوَ إِلَـٰهٌ وَٰحِدٌ وَلِيَذَّكَّرَ أُو۟لُوا۟ ٱلْأَلْبَـٰبِ (٥۲)
This is a proclamation for mankind, so that they may be warned thereby and know that He is indeed the One God, and those who possess intellect may take admonition. (52)

Key Points

The last Ayah of Surah Ibrahim and its first Ayah concern the role of the Quran and its mission in guiding people. In the first Ayah, we read: «كتاب انزلناه الیك لتخرج الناس من الظلمات الى النور» (A Book We have sent down to you so that you may bring mankind out of the darknesses into the light). And in this Ayah, we read: «هذا بلاغ للناس...» (This is a proclamation for mankind...). Furthermore, just as the Quran is a balāgh (proclamation/message), its messenger is also a balāgh, as stated in Ayah 54 of Surah An-Noor: «ما على الرسول الا البلاغ» (The Messenger is only responsible for the proclamation).

Messages

1- The Quran is the source of propagation (Tabligh), and propagators must derive their material for propagation from the Quran. «هذا بلاغ»

2- The Quran is the Book of Monotheism (Tawhid), and a monotheist (muwahhid) must be built from the Quran. «هذا بلاغ ... لیعلموا انّما هو اله واحد»

3- Proclamation (Iblāgh) alone is not sufficient; warning (Indhār) is also necessary. (Indeed, the goal of propagation is warning.) «ولینذروا»

4- Knowing is not enough; attention and remembrance (Tadhakkur) are also necessary. «لیعلموا لیذكّر»

5- Familiarity (Uns) with the Quran provides man with both knowledge, recognition, and insight. «لیعلموا» And through remembrance and counsel, it encourages man toward good deeds. «لیذكّر»

6- The Quran is a proclamation for all people, but only those possessing intellect (ulul-albāb) remember and take counsel from it. «ولیذكّر اولوا الالباب» Indeed, if we pay attention to the guidance of the Quran wholeheartedly, we will become monotheistic (muwahhid) and mindful individuals. Insha’Allah.

Portrait of Surah Al-Hijr

This Surah contains 99 Ayahs and, according to the famous opinion, is the fifty-second Surah revealed to the Noble Prophet صلى الله علیه وآله in Mecca before the Hijra (migration). "Hijr" is the name of a city where the people of Prophet Salih lived, and the name of this Surah is taken from its eightieth Ayah, which concerns the people of Salih: «كذّبت اصحاب الحجر المرسلین» (The dwellers of Al-Hijr denied the Messengers). Most of the content of this Surah concerns the slander and mockery directed by the disbelievers toward the Quran and the Noble Prophet صلى الله علیه وآله, and the command to be patient and forgiving toward them. It serves as a form of comfort and consolation for the dear Prophet against the pressure of the opponents' attacks, following the passing of Lady Khadijah and Hadrat Abu Talib.
